commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an opt-in per-provider rolling-window auto-start setting, scheduler, provider CLI ping commands, persisted config, settings UI status text, and tests for Codex, Claude, and OpenCode.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: OpenCode usage is web-cookie sourced and Claude can use API/OAuth/web, while the scheduler only checks the toggle/token state before running a CLI prompt. I did not run live provider probes because repository policy forbids ad-hoc validation that can touch real accounts or Keychain prompts without explicit request.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor proof plus maintainer auth/product sign-off; automation cannot prove the contributor's provider account setup or decide the permanent provider-source boundary.

target.title: Auto-start rolling window
target.body:
## Summary

Adds an opt-in **Auto-start rolling window** setting for providers whose rolling usage windows can be restarted by sending a tiny prompt through a known CLI harness.

Supported providers:
- Codex
- Claude
- OpenCode

When enabled, CodexBar watches provider refresh results. If the previous snapshot had a rolling window that has ended and the latest provider data shows no active replacement window, CodexBar sends a small prompt through the provider CLI to start the next rolling window, then refreshes that provider again.

## Why

Heavily influenced by Theo's latest video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3sTu8sSLVfg which gave me the idea.

The goal is to always have a 5-hour rolling window active, so that when you do start a line of work that may be more complex or take longer, you don't necessarily start at necessarily the very beginning of that 5-hour window, so you can take advantage of a slightly larger window of burst activity.

## Details

- Adds a per-provider `rollingWindowAutoStartEnabled` config flag, disabled by default
- Adds an `Auto-start rolling window` toggle in supported provider settings
- Starts only one ping per expired reset window and avoids duplicate in-flight starts
- Clears the attempted reset marker on ping failure so a later refresh can retry
- Shows transient status text in provider settings while starting or after failure
- Runs the ping through existing subprocess infrastructure with a 90s timeout
- Uses the default prompt: `Say hi, then stop.`
- Uses cheap/default harness choices:
  - Codex: `codex exec --skip-git-repo-check --ephemeral -m gpt-5.4-mini -c model_reasoning_effort=low`
  - Claude: `claude -p ... --model haiku`
  - OpenCode: `opencode run --model anthropic/claude-3-5-haiku-latest --variant minimal`
- Allows local overrides via `CODEXBAR_ROLLING_WINDOW_<PROVIDER>_PROMPT`, `_TIMEOUT`, `_BINARY`, `_MODEL`, and `_REASONING`

## Notes

This intentionally does not add behavior for providers without a known-enough prompt harness. The scheduler is only exposed for Codex, Claude, and OpenCode.

## Validation

- `make check`
- `swift test --filter RollingWindowAutoStartTests`
target.author: quicksaver
